rulings.Case Title: STEPHAN HOWSEPIAN ET AL VS. SALMAN SHARIAT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 17, 2025 line 1. PETITIONER STEPHAN HOWSEPIAN, JULIA CHOPE MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD is continued to February 19, 2025. Parties to provide courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion filed prior to September 19, 2024 no later than January 31, 2025 with cover letters reflecting new haring date. Additionally, no later than January 31, 2025 opposing party to file a supplemental brief, not to exceed 2 pages, addressing authority allowing for filing of "supplement to respondents opposition" months after the original hearing date and after the opposition having been filed. Order continuing the hearing date did not authorize any additional filings. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LILIAN PADUA ET AL VS. VI THANH LE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce The Terms Of Plaintiffs Acceptance Of Defendant Vi Thanh Le?S Offers To Compromise Under Ccp Section 998 And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant And Her Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 17, 2025 line 4. PLAINTIFF LILIAN PADUA AN INDIVIDUAL, DAVE MORALES NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE THE TERMS OF PLAINTIFFS ACCEPTANCE OF DEFENDANT VI THANH LE?S OFFERS TO COMPROMISE UNDER CCP SECTION 998 AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND HER ATTORNEY is DENIED. No authority is cited for the proposition that the requested relief (for the Court to determine the correct provisions of the Settlement and Release Agreement) is available to the Plaintiffs. Plaintiffs only cited procedural authority is CCP 128(a)(4) without any case authority or analysis of applicability of this section to the 998 offers (e.g. are they "judgments," "orders" or "process?"). Plaintiff further fails to point to any part of the accepted 998 offer requiring the Defendant to agree to a Settlement and Release Agreement on the terms proposed by the Plaintiff. The executed CCP 998 offer includes, inter alia, the terms "plaintiff hereby releases all claims" and "Plaintiff agrees to satisfy all liens ." Those are the terms of the agreement. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
